name: Anaphylaxis
summary: Severe allergic reaction — nuts, stings, medications. Airway swelling can be fatal in minutes.

callEmergency.text: Call 999 immediately. Anaphylaxis can kill within minutes.

steps: If light-headed or faint — lay them down, raise legs if possible.
If airway/breathing problems are the main issue — they may prefer to sit upright (makes breathing easier).
If they have an auto-injector, help them to use it: take cap off, push tip firmly into outer thigh until it clicks, hold for 10 seconds.
Check the specific auto-injector instructions — timings vary slightly between makes.
If not breathing normally — start CPR.

warnings.text: About 3 in 100 people have an allergic reaction to bee stings. This is a medical emergency, not a regular sting.
Auto-injector timing and method varies by brand — read the device if you have time.
